mumbai.title: 50-30-20 Budgeting Rule
mumbai.snippet: 50-30-20 rule: 50% Needs (rent, groceries, EMIs), 30% Wants (dining, travel), 20% Savings (SIP, PPF, emergency fund). Mumbai/Delhi: 55-60% for needs.

sip.title: 50-30-20 Budgeting Rule
sip.snippet: 50-30-20 rule: 50% Needs (rent, groceries, EMIs), 30% Wants (dining, travel), 20% Savings (SIP, PPF, emergency fund). Mumbai/Delhi: 55-60% for needs.

sip.title: SIP Investment Guide
sip.snippet: SIP means fixed monthly auto-debit into mutual funds. Rupee cost averaging means low NAV gives more units. SIP can start from Rs 500/month. LTCG above Rs 1.25L is taxed at 12.5%.

ppf.title: 50-30-20 Budgeting Rule

ppf.title: PPF vs NPS Comparison
ppf.snippet: PPF: 15-year lock-in, around 7.1% returns, EEE tax-free, Rs 1.5L limit, zero risk. NPS: till age 60, 8-12% market-linked, extra Rs 50,000 under 80CCD(1B). Use both: PPF safety plus NPS retirement dedu

ppf.title: PPF vs NPS Comparison
ppf.snippet: PPF: 15-year lock-in, around 7.1% returns, EEE tax-free, Rs 1.5L limit, zero risk. NPS: till age 60, 8-12% market-linked, extra Rs 50,000 under 80CCD(1B). Use both: PPF safety plus NPS retirement dedu

ppf.title: Legal Tax Saving Strategies
ppf.snippet: 80C Rs 1.5L: ELSS, PPF, EPF, home loan principal. 80D Rs 25,000: health insurance. 80CCD(1B) Rs 50,000: NPS extra. 24(b) Rs 2L: home loan interest. <PERSON> common deductions around Rs 4.25L.

ppf.title: Legal Tax Saving Strategies
ppf.snippet: 80C Rs 1.5L: ELSS, PPF, EPF, home loan principal. 80D Rs 25,000: health insurance. 80CCD(1B) Rs 50,000: NPS extra. 24(b) Rs 2L: home loan interest. <PERSON> common deductions around Rs 4.25L.

ppf.title: Retirement Planning India
ppf.snippet: Corpus equals monthly expenses times 12 times 25 using the 4% rule. Rs 50,000/month expenses implies around Rs 1.5 Cr needed. With 25+ years, an example allocation is 70% equity, 20% NPS, and 10% PPF.

ppf.title: Retirement Planning India
ppf.snippet: Corpus equals monthly expenses times 12 times 25 using the 4% rule. Rs 50,000/month expenses implies around Rs 1.5 Cr needed. With 25+ years, an example allocation is 70% equity, 20% NPS, and 10% PPF.

ppf.title: Investment Return Warnings
ppf.snippet: No investment guarantees returns. FD may offer around 6.5-7.5%, PPF around 7.1%, and SGB gives 2.5% plus gold movement. Red flag: any scheme promising more than 12% guaranteed.

40,000/month.title: Emergency Fund Guide
40,000/month.snippet: Target 3-6 months of monthly expenses. Best: liquid mutual funds, 1-day redemption, around 7% returns. Rs 40,000/month expenses means target Rs 1.2L to Rs 2.4L.

1-day.title: Emergency Fund Guide
1-day.snippet: Target 3-6 months of monthly expenses. Best: liquid mutual funds, 1-day redemption, around 7% returns. Rs 40,000/month expenses means target Rs 1.2L to Rs 2.4L.

1.25l.title: ELSS Tax-Saving Funds
1.25l.snippet: ELSS: 3-year lock-in, deduction up to Rs 1.5L under 80C. Returns 12-15% CAGR over 5+ years. LTCG above Rs 1.25L taxed at 12.5%.

1.25l.title: SIP Investment Guide
1.25l.snippet: SIP means fixed monthly auto-debit into mutual funds. Rupee cost averaging means low NAV gives more units. SIP can start from Rs 500/month. LTCG above Rs 1.25L is taxed at 12.5%.

87a.title: Income Tax Slabs FY 2024-25 New Regime
87a.snippet: Rs 0-3L NIL, Rs 3-7L 5%, Rs 7-10L 10%, Rs 10-12L 15%, Rs 12-15L 20%, above Rs 15L 30%. 87A rebate: up to Rs 7L equals zero tax. Standard deduction Rs 75,000 for salaried.

sebi.title: Ponzi Scheme Warning Signs
sebi.snippet: Warning signs include guaranteed high returns, no SEBI or RBI registration, and pressure to recruit. Investors can report issues at scores.sebi.gov.in.

rbi.title: Ponzi Scheme Warning Signs
